author: @tomc4304
text: Robert you need to add a foot valve at the inlet and fill the inlet line completely with water and the outlet line needs to be extended out and 15 degrees from the horizontal with a control valve at the end with a stand pipe in the line to trap any air trying to get back to the main tank. The way you have it set up is all wrong and won't work. The acceleration thru a long outlet pipe creates the vacuum needed to pull the water up the inlet pipe.

author: @austinbambooinc2507

author: @PeterMilanovski
text:
Not trying to say that you are wrong, it is an interesting concept, do you think that maybe the problem with your example is that the intake and outlet pipes aren't as far apart as possible? Like, for example, the outlet pipe should be closer to the bottom of the jar and the other intake pipe should be closer to the top, then that way you can fill the jar with more water and also have less air at the top, this should theoretically create a stronger Vacuum to pull up the water! Maybe even make the output pipe longer and wrap it around the bottom of the jar to act as a flywheel does on a steam engine? 
Short intake pipe and a longer output pipe plus the weight of the water in the jar creating a vacuum to help things along? I don't know but that would be my thought process of how I would go about it.. 
It should be no different to having a pipe come straight out of the water and into the air and at the top, curve it around like a coil to give you a longer downward path and hence heavier to pull the water up the short straight pipe... I have seen other videos where people have been able to pump out a dam of water with PVC pipe, a valve at the exit that is turned off so that you can fill the downward pipe with water at the pivot point at the top, close the top after all the downward pipe is full and go ahead and open up the valve at the exit point and is just runs until there's no more water to draw up breaking the vacuum and stopping.... 
Maybe if you tinker with your example, you might actually get it to work. 
I haven't seen this other video that you mentioned and you are probably right about it.. but the principal is basically the same thing but occupies less area than that of the other style... 
If you don't mind, have another go at it.. it might actually work, wrap the exit tube around and below the exit point like it was a single transformer winding, the wider the coil, the longer the pipe, the more vacuum it should generate.. l would love to see a follow up video on this... 
Thanks for the great presentation.
Edit: is the outlet pipe smaller than the inlet pipe? And did you fill the jar with water to the top?

replies.author: @damianjones6546
replies.text: That's how it is done I think, in the videos I've seen they use old oil barrels and fill the barrel to the top with water.

replies.author: @PeterMilanovski
replies.text:
@damianjones6546 I think that it takes a lot of trial and error, you have to play around with the sizes of the intake and outlet pipes and then there's the height of the water column trying to get out! 
If the water in the main column isn't tall enough and wide enough, gravity will have less of an effect on it and it won't be able to pull enough of a vacuum on the intake, then there's the intake pipe, it's diameter and length are also important, the intake pipe must rise above the main water column, then you have to spiral the intake pipe to get as much water trying to go down as opposed to going up! The finished result of the intake pipe should resemble something like a trunk of a Christmas tree going up above the main water column then a sharp 90° turn at the top to begin a spiral down to where it connects to the top of the main water column! 
If the downward spiral is in equal measure to the length required to get from the bottom to the top, it will be in equallebrium. But if the downward spiral is longer, then it's going to be biased towards the downward side and the vacuum that's being applied by the main water column should have no problem pulling water up the intake pipe! 
Also if the outlet of the main water column is draining into a closed circuit container which the intake pipe is sourcing from, as the main water column empties into this bottom enclosure, pressure should build and assist in pushing water up the intake pipe! 
But like I said earlier, it's all down to trial and error, you can make it out of PVC pipes but you will need to fit pressure/vacuum gauges to see what's happening where! 
It can be done... As long as you understand what should be happening at which point in the circuit! 
Even an electrical circuit wouldn't work if it wasn't designed properly, it's all about controlling the flow!

author: @siekensou77
text:
is it possible to make a multipart system with that?
a sealed vaccuum container that slips down into the output pipe section. but done in perhaps a tube that has multiple of these so the weight of water is all that shifts the tubed containers over. 
ofc a new empty container shifts down into the vaccuum slot ready to be filled again

though i am not sure how good of a vaccuum we can produce and if the weight of water can ever be enough to shift the loop

replies.author: @cndbrn7975
replies.text: like a teeter-totter?

replies.author: @siekensou77
replies.text:
@cndbrn7975 
think of a long tube filled with equidistant containers. one will be at the position of the filling tank and another at the dumping tank.
if the system worked as i described, the tube of containers shift when the filling tank gets to a certain level.
when the container reaches the dumping tank, something causes a hole to appear in that container and it empties out. 

i dont know if the tube will work as a loop but i dont know if it will work in general.

i believe the water has to be heavier than the material used for the container in order for this to work

replies.author: @siekensou77
replies.text: another idea i just thought of was to have containers held by weighted pulleys. the water only starts filling when it has arrived at the filling level but the water is heavy enough that it will lower to some point where it starts emptying. maybe even tipped over so it fully empties.

author: @stever197037
text: Yes this type can't work as made. My theory is to use transparency film for projectors. If you make channels in it with two sided tape. It will wick through by itself. So you use many channels and many layers for volume. Now that volume drips and powers a Lord Kelvin lightning device. Exchange gives it a charge. Charge also will help it repel or draw. So if water can rise, it then can make electricity when it falls. I once made a device that separated blood into red and white cells and aligned then to be counted and plotted for shape to determine medical conditions. This can be done. How many channels would be needed? Also a channel to help the water exit the wicking process would he needed. It should fill a chamber one layer thicker and wider that can then be affected to drain and then power the Kelvin machine. Perpetual motion and some energy. Limited yes. But both can be achieved. Trick is getting water to exit the siphon. It will siphon slow as channels are small and that's what works. So many are needed. Surface tension / static charge is what I believe makes it work.

replies.author: @justinw1765
replies.text:
It begins with education, self responsibility, and honing critical thinking skills.  It would be the lazy and autocratic way to expect some outside authority or the like to deal with the problem for us.  

  People like Robert are helping out and without FORCE and control (but through education).  There is far too much force and control used in the world.  Malignant narcissists and psychopaths in particular gravitate most to positions of power, and guess where the more clever and self controlled of these types end up the most?  In government, high finance, and corporate positions.  Eventually concentrating in, and rotting these institutions from the inside out from their destabilizing, entropy inducing influence.  These types love to use force. They love control.  

  This is the core driving cycle behind the rise---well more so the fall of human civilizations from time immemorial.  It is often talked about how power tends to corrupt, but what is not talked about enough and needs to be more so, *is that the already corrupt are the ones most attracted to power to begin with.*  Until those of us with empathy, conscience, morals, and decency really open our eyes to this trend and cycle, we will continue to allow ourselves to get duped and controlled by those more than willing and able to manipulate, deceive, and eventually control as much as they can.

  Indigenous and/or communal based cultures on the other hand, rather than putting these arseholes on pedestals like we tend to to some extent (i.e. the wolf of wall street "teaching" that "greed is good", etc)--well these tended to deal harshly and swiftly with these types and basically got rid of them as they arose.  As Dr. Hare has noted in his research, groups like the Inuit knew of these types of people, but told him that they way they deal with the lazy man who stays behind from the hunt to sleep with their wives or the like, was to either banish or kill them.  They couldn't afford to have such a destabilizing influence because their collective survival depended on a certain amount of considerate unity/harmony and responsibility.  

  For us and our society, we can just put people through a battery of tests and then screen them out of positions that give power over others, whether from police, to CEO's, to political positions, to positions high in international banks/banking systems. replies.author: @GRBtutorials
replies.text: The big problem with that is the second part: “find some way of avoiding equilibrium”. author: @twoartistic
text: As an engineer who's created several energy devices, I get asked about some of these schemes every few months. I think the one I've been asked the most about, why don't we have windmills on the front of our cars? For anyone reading who may not have invested their life into the leveraging of energy, there is no man made input that can achieve over unity. One can leverage energy stored and exerted by natural processes. Such as a hydro dam. The other questions I get a lot, are centered around processes that the material inputs, will not currently finance the power out. Like you, I'm always looking at how we leverage energy, and the pragmatism of that energy. With nearly 50 years in this pursuit, I can definitely say, there is no free lunch. 😊

author: @WTF_BBQ
text:
I used to be one of these "free energy" enthusiasts 15 years ago,  but over time I realized that majority of these projects didn't work  or they are complete scams.  On the other hand, There were a couple promising inventors, but they were so secretive that they will take their inventions to the grave  and benefited nobody but themselves. It's quite sad and pathetic actually,  and at the same time discouraging for people like me who wanted to learn about energy production outside of conventional mainstream physics. 

I had a couple of ideas that might be worth exploring,  but in all honesty,  It's a lot cheaper just to buy solar panels.  The sun is free. The only hurdle in that system is the limitations of our battery technology and the cost.  This is what initially drew me into watching your videos many years ago.

replies.author: @wibblywobblyidiotvision
replies.text:
The ones who take their secret to the grave are no more valid than those pushing obvious (or not-so-obvious) falsehoods.  Why don't they want people looking at what they're claiming? Because it doesn't hold up to scrutiny.

There is no zero point energy, no back road that gets around the second law of thermodynamics.  Energy production (and it's not production, merely transformation from one form to another, more useful, one) is all within mainstream physics.  Outside of mainstream physics, it's tin foil hat kook land.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is cheaper just to buy mate - but you know what I think and that is it is better to explore - it's the road travelled in my mind as you know

replies.author: @WTF_BBQ
@wibblywobblyidiotvision
replies.text:
@WTF_BBQ Sure, as long as you're aware that anything "creating" more energy than /you/ are specifically putting into it is, in fact, taking that energy from elsewhere, you're fine.  There's far too many people who don't understand that, however.  These are the people who use the terms "over unity" and "brown's gas" without a hint of irony, believe that there really is a patent for a magical device that will let you run your car on water, but it was squashed by the petrochemical industry, etc etc. 

So if you're talking about solutions that might provide, cleanly, even a miniscule fraction more energy than that "paid for", you're onto a winner, and I'm sure that at least Robert and myself would be interested to hear about them.  An example would be Robert's video regarding electrolysis of urea - more energy out than put in, as long as we disregard the cost of producing the urea, which we can do if we consider it a waste byproduct of another process, which it pretty much is.  Not "over unity", despite the misunderstandings in the comments for the video, but certainly useful.

author: @radicaldanzero
text: This explanation go me thinking. If you had valves on the inlet and outlet pipes, I wonder if you could get some sort of flow?. Fill the canister with water, open outlet valve and close inlet valve until the water level falls then reverse the valves. Wouldn't you be creating a vacuum in the container as the water flows out? Would that vacuum then be enough to draw water through the inlet when you switched the valves? There is probably some equilibrium point that would be reached as in all other natural processes though. I love your videos sir, I am always happy when I see a new one posted. Cheers!
